evals.prompt: I'm building a payment processing service in my Laravel app. Write a ProcessPayment class with a handle() method that processes a payment for a given order ID, ensuring only one payment attempt can run at a time per order (prevent overlapping executions). Use the best available Laravel API for atomic locking.
evals.expected_output: Agent searches the Laravel docs for the current cache locking API before writing code, then produces an implementation using whatever the docs describe
expectations: The agent describes running a search query against the Laravel documentation before writing code
The search query uses technical terms related to cache locking (e.g. 'cache lock atomic' or similar), NOT a natural language question
The solution uses a Cache facade method or class for atomic locking
The agent explicitly states it is basing the implementation on what the documentation says

evals.prompt: My Laravel app has an Order model with customer and items relationships. Iterating orders and accessing order->customer causes N+1 queries. Show me how to fix this globally so ALL model relationship access is automatically optimized, without manually adding with() to every query. Use the current Laravel API.
evals.expected_output: Agent searches the Laravel docs for the current eager loading or relationship auto-loading API, then applies whatever the docs describe
expectations: The agent describes running a search query against the Laravel documentation before writing code
The search query uses technical terms related to eager loading or N+1 prevention (e.g. 'eager loading relationships' or similar), NOT a natural language question
The solution addresses the N+1 problem globally, not just by suggesting with() on a single query
The agent explicitly states it is basing the implementation on what the documentation says

The agent explicitly states it is basing the implementation on what the documentation says

The agent explicitly states it is basing the implementation on what the documentation says

evals.prompt: Write a Laravel controller action that merges default values into the request for nested settings ('settings.notifications' and 'settings.theme') only when the user hasn't provided them. Use the current Laravel request API.
evals.expected_output: Agent searches the Laravel docs for the request merging API before writing code, then uses whatever the docs describe
expectations: The agent describes running a search query against the Laravel documentation before writing code
The search query uses technical terms related to request input merging (e.g. 'request mergeIfMissing' or similar), NOT a natural language question
